questions.text: What does ECB stand for in the context of block cipher modes?
Which block cipher mode processes each block independently without any chaining?
In CBC mode, what is the input for the first block's encryption?
options: The plaintext block XORed with the previous ciphertext block
The plaintext block XORed with the Initialization Vector (IV)

questions.text: Which of the following block cipher modes can be used as a stream cipher without padding?
What is the main disadvantage of using ECB mode?
options: It requires a very large key
Identical plaintext blocks produce identical ciphertext blocks
It cannot be used with AES

questions.text: Which padding scheme fills the remaining bytes with the count of padding bytes?
Which padding scheme starts with 0x80 followed by zeros?
In the context of block cipher modes, what is an IV (Initialization Vector)?
options: A secret key used for encryption
A random value used to ensure identical plaintexts produce different ciphertexts

questions.text: Which of the following are true about the Counter (CTR) mode?
options: It allows random access to encrypted blocks
It requires only the encryption algorithm

questions.text: What is the block size of AES?
Which of the following are key design principles of a Feistel cipher?
What is the effective key length of DES?
Which of the following are true about the Triple-DES (3DES) with three keys?
options: It is defined as C = E(K3, D(K2, E(K1, P)))
It has an effective key length of 168 bits
It is backward compatible with DES
It is vulnerable to the same meet-in-the-middle attack as double encryption

questions.text: What is the main purpose of the 'Tweak' in XTS-AES?
options: To act as a second secret key
To provide variability without needing to be secret

questions.text: Which of the following block cipher modes are vulnerable to bit-flipping attacks if the IV is not integrity-protected?
In a Feistel cipher, what is the purpose of the final swap operation?
options: To make encryption and decryption use the same algorithm
To add confusion to the output
To ensure the block size is correct

questions.text: Which of the following are true about the Strict Avalanche Criterion (SAC)?
options: Each output bit should change with probability 1/2 when any input bit is flipped
It requires that all output bits change simultaneously
It is a property of the key schedule

questions.text: Which padding schemes add an entire additional block when the message is already a multiple of the block size?
What is the block size of the Data Encryption Standard (DES)?
Which of the following are true about the Output Feedback (OFB) mode?
options: It generates a keystream independent of the plaintext
It is a stream cipher mode
A single bit error in the ciphertext only affects the corresponding plaintext bit

questions.text: Which of the following are valid key sizes for AES?
In Cipher Text Stealing (CTS), what is the key property?
options: The ciphertext is always longer than the plaintext
The ciphertext is the same length as the plaintext
It requires an additional padding block
It can only be used with OFB mode

questions.text: Which of the following are true about the Meet-in-the-Middle attack?
options: It requires a known plaintext-ciphertext pair
It reduces the effort from 2^112 to 2^56 for double DES
It makes triple DES with two keys as secure as triple DES with three keys

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes the avalanche effect in DES?
options: A single bit change in the plaintext affects only the first block
A single bit change in the plaintext or key affects approximately half the ciphertext bits
A single bit change in the key affects only the last round
A single bit change in the plaintext affects only the corresponding output bit

questions.text: Which of the following are true about the Rijndael algorithm (AES)?
options: It was selected by NIST as the AES standard
It uses a Feistel network structure
It supports variable block sizes in its general form
It was the only finalist considered sufficiently secure
